title: albizia tenuiflora
intro: Albizia tenuiflora, also known as the zebrawood or white siris, is a deciduous tree native to Asia, characterized by its unique bark and fragrant flowers. The bark and leaves of Albizia tenuiflora are used in traditional medicine, possessing anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Albizia tenuiflora include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, or dried and processed into capsules or tablets for consumption.
